codeReview.summary: The commit adds a null-children guard and test, but it does not actually remove the spacer from the real header-only Results panels because those callsites still pass empty `<div />` children.

issues.description: `hasBody` only excludes `null`/`undefined`/`false`. Existing header-only callsites pass an empty React element (`<div />`), which still evaluates as a body and keeps rendering the `mt-5` spacer.
The read-only header-only `SectionCard` still passes `<div />` as children, so the body spacer remains present instead of being eliminated.
The save-result header-only `SectionCard` still passes `<div />` as children, so the body spacer remains present instead of being eliminated.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill requires explicit red→green TDD (failing test before implementation), but this session's transcript skeleton shows code patches before the first targeted test run while `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` is still true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session `4d1e1639-1d17-4470-ad4c-cc5a5284a5a8`: `ApplyPatch` calls precede `npm test -- --run src/components/ui/SectionCard.test.tsx`; handoff `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` is true.

summary: Fail: spacer-removal behavior is not achieved in the actual header-only ResultsStep SectionCard usages because placeholder children remain and still trigger the body wrapper.
